<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  .form-chip{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:4px;padding:2px 8px;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.88rem;margin:1px;color:var(--yellow)}
  .genus-chip{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:4px;
    border-radius:6px;padding:3px 10px;font-size:.87rem;margin:2px}
  .genus-0{background:#1a2030;border:1px solid var(--blue);color:var(--blue)}
  .genus-1{background:#2a1830;border:1px solid var(--violet);color:var(--violet)}
  .genus-2{background:#1a2818;border:1px solid var(--green);color:var(--green)}
  .genus-3{background:#2a1818;border:1px solid var(--pink);color:var(--pink)}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- display-prefs-head-auto-begin -->
<script src="./js/display-prefs.js"></script>
<!-- display-prefs-head-auto-end -->
</head>
rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Binary forms 2 Reduction 3 Class group 4 Genus theory 5 Ideals vs forms

Quadratic forms & genus theory

Gauss's Disquisitiones Arithmeticae (1801) classifies binary quadratic forms $ax^2+bxy+cy^2$ by discriminant and equivalence. The resulting form class group, Gauss composition, and genus theory constitute both a computational masterpiece and the historical seed of modern algebraic number theory — every prime represented by a given form is governed by a congruence condition that traces directly back to Legendre symbols and, ultimately, to ideal class groups of imaginary quadratic fields.

blocks.html:
1. Binary quadratic forms

A binary quadratic form is a homogeneous degree-2 polynomial in two variables: $$ f(x,y) = ax^2 + bxy + cy^2, \qquad a,b,c \in \mathbb{Z}. $$ The single most important invariant is the discriminant $$ D = b^2 - 4ac. $$ The sign of $D$ determines the character of $f$: if $D < 0$ and $a > 0$ then $f(x,y) > 0$ for all $(x,y)\ne(0,0)$ (positive-definite); if $D > 0$ then $f$ takes both positive and negative values (indefinite).

Two forms $f$ and $g$ are properly equivalent if $g(x,y) = f(px+qy,\,rx+sy)$ for some matrix $\begin{pmatrix}p&q\\r&s\end{pmatrix} \in \mathrm{GL}_2^+(\mathbb{Z})$ (determinant $+1$). Equivalence preserves $D$ and the set of integers represented by $f$. A form is primitive if $\gcd(a,b,c)=1$; primitivity is also preserved.

Why discriminants? Completing the square gives $4af(x,y) = (2ax+by)^2 - Dy^2$. So for $D < 0$ the form is positive-definite (for $a>0$), and the root structure of the associated quadratic $at^2+bt+c$ has $D$ as the classical discriminant. The same $D$ governs which number field $\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{D})$ the form "lives over."

What integers does a form represent?

We say $f$ represents an integer $n$ if $f(x,y)=n$ for some $(x,y)\in\mathbb{Z}^2$. Two equivalent forms represent exactly the same integers. The key question of genus theory is: for which primes $p$ does a given form represent $p$? The answer will depend only on $p$ modulo $|D|$, a fact equivalent to quadratic reciprocity.

params.title: Discriminant explorer
Discriminant explorer

blocks.html:
See also
  • The Legendre symbol

Used in
Quadratic forms & genus theory · Reduction theory

2. Reduction theory

For positive-definite forms ($D < 0$, $a > 0$), Gauss showed every equivalence class contains a unique reduced form satisfying: $$ |b| \le a \le c, \quad \text{and} \quad b \ge 0 \text{ if } |b| = a \text{ or } a = c. $$ This turns the set of equivalence classes into a finite, computable list. The number of such classes, the class number $h(D)$, depends only on $D$. For example, $h(-4)=1$, $h(-23)=3$, $h(-163)=1$ (a deep fact connected to Heegner numbers).

The reduction algorithm

Starting from any primitive positive-definite form $(a,b,c)$ with $D = b^2-4ac$:

  1. If $a > c$, swap $a \leftrightarrow c$ and negate $b$ (which replaces $f(x,y)$ by $f(y,x)$).
  2. If $|b| > a$, replace $b$ by $b - 2ak$ where $k = \lfloor (b+a)/(2a) \rfloor$; update $c$ via $c \leftarrow (b'^2 - D)/(4a)$ using new $b' = b-2ak$.
  3. Repeat until the inequalities hold.

This algorithm terminates because at each step $a$ strictly decreases or the pair $(a,|b|)$ improves lexicographically.

params.title: Form reducer
Form reducer

blocks.html:
Class numbers for small $|D|$

The widget below lists all reduced forms of a chosen fundamental discriminant $D < 0$, giving a concrete picture of the class group before we make it algebraic.

params.title: Reduced forms lister
params.hint: select $D$ to see all reduced primitive forms

params.title: Reduced forms lister
params.hint: select $D$ to see all reduced primitive forms

blocks.html:
Used in
Quadratic forms & genus theory · Form class group

3. The form class group

The set $\mathrm{Cl}(D)$ of proper equivalence classes of primitive forms of discriminant $D$ is more than a set — it carries a group structure discovered by Gauss (via composition of forms) and elegantly repackaged by Dirichlet. The Dirichlet composition of two primitive forms $f_1,f_2$ of discriminant $D$ is the unique primitive form $f_1 * f_2$ of discriminant $D$ such that: $$ f_1(x_1,y_1)\cdot f_2(x_2,y_2) = (f_1*f_2)\bigl(B(x_1,x_2,y_1,y_2),\, C(x_1,x_2,y_1,y_2)\bigr) $$ for explicit bilinear forms $B$ and $C$. This composition descends to an abelian group law on $\mathrm{Cl}(D)$.

The identity element is the class of the principal form: $$ f_0 = \begin{cases} x^2 - \tfrac{D}{4}y^2 & \text{if } 4\mid D, \\ x^2 + xy - \tfrac{D-1}{4}y^2 & \text{if } D \equiv 1\pmod{4}. \end{cases} $$ The inverse of the class of $(a,b,c)$ is the class of $(a,-b,c)$ — simply negate the middle coefficient.

A finite abelian group. $|\mathrm{Cl}(D)| = h(D)$, the class number. By Minkowski's bound, every class contains a form with $a \le \sqrt{|D|/3}$, so $h(D)$ is finite. Its growth is predicted by the Brauer–Siegel theorem: roughly $h(D) \sim \sqrt{|D|}$ on average.

Group structure visualizer

For $D = -23$ the class group $\mathrm{Cl}(-23) \cong \mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z}$ has three elements: the three reduced forms $(1,1,6)$, $(2,1,3)$, $(2,-1,3)$. Squaring the non-principal element cycles back to the identity.

params.title: Class group of $D = -23$
params.hint: see how composition cycles through the three classes

blocks.html:
Used in
Quadratic forms & genus theory · Genus theory

4. Genus theory

Gauss observed that the form class group could be further partitioned into genera: classes that are indistinguishable by congruence conditions. A genus character for a prime $p \mid D$ is a homomorphism $$ \chi_p : \mathrm{Cl}(D) \to \{\pm 1\}, \qquad \chi_p([f]) = \left(\frac{m}{p}\right) $$ where $m$ is any integer represented by $f$ with $\gcd(m,D)=1$. The value is well-defined on the class (independent of which $m$ we pick) by the theory of the Legendre symbol.

The collection of all genus characters combines into a single homomorphism $$ \Phi : \mathrm{Cl}(D) \longrightarrow (\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})^{t-1}, $$ where $t$ is the number of prime discriminant divisors of $D$ (odd primes $p \mid D$ plus corrections for the factor of $4$ or $8$ at $2$). The image of $\Phi$ is all of $(\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})^{t-1}$, so the number of genera is exactly $2^{t-1}$, and each genus has the same number of classes.

The principal genus. $\ker\Phi$ is the principal genus: it equals the subgroup of squares $\mathrm{Cl}(D)^2 = \{[f]^2 : [f] \in \mathrm{Cl}(D)\}$. The quotient $\mathrm{Cl}(D)/\mathrm{Cl}(D)^2 \cong (\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})^{t-1}$ is the group of genera.

Ambiguous classes

A class $[f]$ is ambiguous if $[f] = [f]^{-1}$, i.e., if $[f]$ has order $\le 2$ in $\mathrm{Cl}(D)$. The reduced representative $(a,b,c)$ of an ambiguous class satisfies $b=0$, $b=a$, or $a=c$. The number of ambiguous classes is $2^{t-1}$ — exactly the number of genera. This is no coincidence: every genus contains exactly one ambiguous class.

See also
  • Quadratic reciprocity law

Used in
Quadratic forms & genus theory · Forms and ideal classes
Sums of squares · Legendre three-square theorem

params.title: Genus partition
params.hint: select a discriminant to see classes sorted by genus

blocks.html:
5. Forms and ideal classes

The deepest connection in this theory is the identification of the form class group with the ideal class group of an imaginary quadratic field. Fix a fundamental discriminant $D < 0$ and let $K = \mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{D})$. The ring of integers $\mathcal{O}_K$ is $\mathbb{Z}[\tfrac{1+\sqrt{D}}{2}]$ if $D \equiv 1 \pmod{4}$, and $\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{D}]$ otherwise.

The bijection. To a primitive form $(a,b,c)$ of discriminant $D$, associate the $\mathcal{O}_K$-ideal $$ \mathfrak{a} = a\mathbb{Z} + \frac{-b+\sqrt{D}}{2}\mathbb{Z}. $$ This construction sends proper equivalence classes of forms to ideal classes, and the Dirichlet composition corresponds exactly to ideal multiplication. The result is a canonical group isomorphism: $$ \mathrm{Cl}(D) \xrightarrow{\;\sim\;} \mathrm{Cl}(K). $$

Under this isomorphism, the principal genus corresponds to the principal genus of ideals — ideals in the principal genus are exactly those whose ideal class lies in the kernel of all genus characters, which equals $\mathrm{Cl}(K)^2$, the subgroup of squares.

See also
  • Ideal factorization and ramification

Primes represented by the principal form

An odd prime $p$ not dividing $D$ is represented by the principal form of discriminant $D$ if and only if the prime ideal $\mathfrak{p}$ above $p$ in $\mathcal{O}_K$ is a principal ideal. By class field theory, this happens if and only if $p$ splits completely in the Hilbert class field $H/K$ — the maximal unramified abelian extension of $K$, which has degree $[H:K] = h(D)$. The primes represented by the principal form are exactly those in a specific set of congruence classes modulo $|D|$, determined by a product of Legendre symbols. This is the final link back to quadratic residues:

A prime $p$ (with $p \nmid D$) is represented by the principal form $\Leftrightarrow$ $\left(\frac{D}{p}\right) = 1$ (splits in $K$) and $p$'s Frobenius in $\mathrm{Gal}(H/K)$ is trivial — equivalently, $p$ splits completely in the Hilbert class field.

params.title: Which primes are represented?
params.hint: by the principal form of given discriminant

params.title: Which primes are represented?
params.hint: by the principal form of given discriminant

blocks.html:
Class field theory in miniature

The isomorphism $\mathrm{Cl}(D) \cong \mathrm{Gal}(H/K)$ (Artin isomorphism restricted to $K$) makes the Hilbert class field $H$ the "universal" field where every ideal class becomes principal. Gauss's genus theory then says: there is an intermediate field $K \subset K^{\mathrm{gen}} \subset H$ such that $$ \mathrm{Gal}(K^{\mathrm{gen}}/K) \cong \mathrm{Cl}(D)/\mathrm{Cl}(D)^2 \cong (\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})^{t-1}. $$ This field $K^{\mathrm{gen}}$ is the genus field, an explicitly constructible abelian extension of $K$. Gauss's genus theory is thus a concrete preview of the main theorem of class field theory, proved over a century later.

The full class field theory proof of $\mathrm{Cl}(D) \cong \mathrm{Cl}(K)$ uses the fact that every fractional ideal of $\mathcal{O}_K$ can be written in the form above, plus that two such ideals are in the same class iff the corresponding forms are properly equivalent. The key identity linking norms of ideals to values of forms is $N(\mathfrak{a}) = a$, where $a$ is the leading coefficient.

</section>
